reasoning: The giveaway was initially challenged, but the Philadelphia court allowed it to continue in November 2024. Wisconsin courts rejected efforts by the AG to block it in early 2025. A federal class-action civil lawsuit (McAferty v. Musk) was filed, and in August 2025, a federal judge ordered Musk to face the suit (denying a motion to dismiss). Civil lawsuits typically take years to reach a final judgment, meaning there almost certainly will not be a final ruling of illegality by December 31, 2025. Furthermore, an August 2025 article in the Election Law Blog explicitly notes that Musk "has faced no other consequences" and is unlikely to be penalized by the deadlocked FEC or the Trump-era DOJ. Without any successful regulatory action or rapid court ruling, the chances of it being formally ruled illegal before the end of the year are near zero.
